evidence: The amber probe and the four lilac score bars form one covariation system; the probe and the peaking bar clearly lead while candidates recede in muted periwinkle.
The scene reads as a single mechanism (probe + four candidates + their score bars), not five unrelated novel ideas competing at equal weight.
c1..c4 sit at each arrow tip, 'probe' rides the amber arrow, and each score bar is labeled directly above with its readout below; no legend or key is present.
The only prose is the short instruction 'Turn the probe (drag it or use arrow keys) until a bar peaks,' which annotates the visible interactive rather than replacing it.
Candidate labels, the probe angle readout, and the mono score digits (+34, +94, -77, etc.) remain readable at 375, 768, and 1280 px despite being intentionally quiet.
The probe arrow, candidate arrows, and all bars sit fully inside the frame with margin at every viewport, including the extended probe in the aligned frame.
In orthogonal-1280 the large amber probe arrowhead at 110° overlaps and partially obscures the 'c3' candidate label at the top-left.
suggestion: Offset the c3 label outward or shorten the probe arrowhead so it never covers a candidate label when the probe rotates onto that direction.

evidence: Token semantics hold: amber = attention/probe, periwinkle = observed candidate arrows, lilac = parameter score bars, cream labels; no inverted or stock-primary colors appear.
Every element carries content — arrows, labels, bars, readouts, and the reference circle for the unit directions; no mascots or ornaments.

Each driven state matches intent: initial probe at 90° with balanced bars, aligned c1 peaks at +100, orthogonal c3 peaks with c1 at 0, and anti-aligned shows c1 -100 with its bar below the zero baseline.
Score bars sit in a labeled row beneath the diagram; the shared c1..c4 labels keep each bar tied to its candidate arrow without much cross-frame hunting.
The alternate driven states read as continuous covariation of bar height and sign with probe angle rather than disjoint cuts.
The composition has generous margins and a clear top-diagram, bottom-bars reading order at all three widths.
